is_secure.note: When true, uses SecureText attr instead of Text

NTypeInto.description: Type a string into a UI element by simulating keystrokes, with optional secure-text masking and empty-field handling.
NTypeInto.use_when: User wants to fill a single text input by typing characters one at a time. For checkboxes use NCheck; for dropdowns use NSelectItem; for clipboard-paste fills use NSetText.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The field already accepts a direct value-set and you do not need keystroke-level fidelity (faster, no focus needed).
The value is large or contains characters the target does not accept via keystrokes; paste from the clipboard instead.

NClick.description: Click a UI element with configurable click type (Single/Double) and mouse button (Left/Right/Middle).
NClick.use_when: User wants a single left click on a button or element. For double-clicks prefer NDoubleClick; for right-clicks prefer NRightClick; for hold-and-drag use NDragAndDrop.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The target is a checkbox or radio button and you need to set its state instead of just clicking.
The target is a combo-box or list and you need to choose an item by visible text rather than firing a click.
You only need to verify the element's state without interacting; this just observes, NClick acts.

NCheck.description: Set a checkbox or radio button to a Check, Uncheck, or Toggle state.
NCheck.use_when: User wants to set a checkbox/radio to a known state. For arbitrary clicks use NClick; for verifying current state without changing it use NCheckState.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The target is a generic button or link rather than a checkbox/radio; a raw click is enough.
The control is a dropdown/list rather than a check or radio toggle.
You only need to read whether the box is checked, not change it.

cursor_motion_type.note: When non-default, uses fully qualified enum: [UiPath.UIAutomationNext.Enums.CursorMotionType.{value}]

NHover.description: Hover the mouse over a UI element to trigger hover-only behaviours (tooltips, menus).
NHover.use_when: User wants to surface hover-triggered UI (tooltips, fly-out menus) without clicking. For clicking use NClick.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You only want a visual debug outline, not a real mouse hover that fires the application's hover handlers.
You need a saved image of the element rather than to interact with it.

selector.note: When empty, self-closing tag with no Target child; when provided, has Target child

NKeyboardShortcuts.Target.note: Only emitted when selector is non-empty; otherwise self-closing element

NKeyboardShortcuts.description: Send a keyboard shortcut chord (modifier keys + key) to a UI element.
NKeyboardShortcuts.use_when: User wants to invoke a keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+S, Alt+F4, etc.) on the target element. For typing literal text use NTypeInto.

NDoubleClick.note: Convenience wrapper over gen_nclick with click_type=Double
NDoubleClick.description: Double-click a UI element with the chosen mouse button.
NDoubleClick.use_when: User explicitly wants a double click. For single click use NClick; for right click use NRightClick.

NRightClick.note: Convenience wrapper over gen_nclick with mouse_button=Right
NRightClick.description: Right-click a UI element to invoke its context menu.
NRightClick.use_when: User wants to open a context menu. For ordinary clicks use NClick; for double-clicks use NDoubleClick.

selector.note: Provide exactly one of selector or in_ui_element

in_ui_element.note: When provided, self-closing tag (no Target child)

NGetText.Target.note: Only emitted when selector is provided (not in_ui_element mode)

NGetText.description: Read the visible text of a UI element into an output variable.
NGetText.use_when: User wants to capture the text shown on screen for an element. For OCR-only surfaces use GetOCRText; for clipboard use NGetClipboard; for browser DOM data use NGetBrowserData.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The source already lives on the system clipboard (e.g. user-copied text) rather than inside a UI element.
The source is structured browser data (cookies, storage, form values) rather than rendered text on an element.

NCheckState.IfExists.content: Sequence with DisplayName='Target appears', contains if_exists_body and viewstate_block

NCheckState.IfNotExists.content: Sequence with DisplayName='Target does not appear', contains if_not_exists_body and viewstate_block

NCheckState.description: Inspect whether a UI element appears, disappears, or matches a state, returning the result without modifying the UI.
NCheckState.use_when: User wants to test the presence/state of an element as a Boolean. For deciding which branch runs use this together with If; to actively check a checkbox use NCheck.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You need to act on the element rather than just observe its state.
You need to actually toggle the checkbox/radio rather than read its state.
You need to assert the element's existence/visibility rather than its checked state.

static_items.note: List of static option strings for the Items scg:List child element

NSelectItem.Items.content: scg:List x:TypeArguments='x:String' with static item x:String elements

NSelectItem.description: Select an item from a combobox/dropdown by visible text.
NSelectItem.use_when: User wants to pick a value from a dropdown/combobox. For multi-select lists use SelectMultipleItems; for typing into the field use NTypeInto.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The control is a free button or link, not a combo-box/list with named options.
The control is a checkbox or radio button rather than a multi-option selector.
The dropdown is editable and the value is free-form text rather than one of the predefined items.

NMouseScroll.description: Scroll the mouse wheel on a UI element by a number of clicks in a given direction.
NMouseScroll.use_when: User wants to scroll a page or panel via mouse wheel. For keyboard-driven scrolling use NKeyboardShortcuts (PageDown / End).

ApplicationEventTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when a configured application event occurs (wizard-only).
ApplicationEventTrigger.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to configure ApplicationEventTrigger in UiPath Studio's trigger wizard.

ExtractUIData.description: Classic Data Scraping wizard output - extracts tabular UI data based on a recorded pattern (wizard-only).
ExtractUIData.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to use Studio's Data Scraping wizard. The modern equivalent for forms is NFillForm + NExtractFormDataGeneric.

GetAttribute.description: Read a named UI element attribute (innertext, aaname, etc.) into an output variable (wizard-only).
GetAttribute.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to configure GetAttribute through Studio's UI element wizard. For modern web data prefer NGetBrowserData.

GoogleCloudOCR.description: OCR engine that calls Google Cloud Vision against the captured image region.
GoogleCloudOCR.use_when: User wants OCR via Google Cloud and has an API key. For on-device OCR use GoogleOCR (Tesseract); for Azure use MicrosoftAzureComputerVisionOCR.

GoogleOCR.description: Tesseract-based on-device OCR engine (no cloud call).
GoogleOCR.use_when: User wants free, offline OCR. For higher accuracy on noisy images consider GoogleCloudOCR or MicrosoftAzureComputerVisionOCR.

MicrosoftAzureComputerVisionOCR.description: OCR engine that calls Microsoft Azure Computer Vision against the captured image region.
MicrosoftAzureComputerVisionOCR.use_when: User wants OCR via Azure Cognitive Services. For Google use GoogleCloudOCR; for offline use GoogleOCR.

NAccessibilityCheck.description: Run an accessibility audit (axe-core) against the current browser/app state (wizard-only).
NAccessibilityCheck.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to configure NAccessibilityCheck through Studio's accessibility wizard.

NApplicationCard.description: Modern Use Application/Browser scope - opens or attaches to a target application or browser and runs nested activities against it.
NApplicationCard.use_when: User wants the modern Use Application/Browser container that nests all child UI activities under one target. For just opening without nesting use NApplicationCardOpen; for attaching use NApplicationCardAttach.

NBlockUserInput.description: Block the user's mouse and keyboard input until a matching NUnblockUserInput runs.
NBlockUserInput.use_when: User wants to prevent accidental human input during an automation. Always pair with NUnblockUserInput in a Try/Finally.

NBrowserDialogScope.description: Pre-arm handling of a browser dialog (alert/confirm/prompt/beforeunload) that will appear inside the scope.
NBrowserDialogScope.use_when: User wants to auto-accept/dismiss a JS dialog raised by activities inside the scope. For file pickers use NBrowserFilePickerScope.

NBrowserFilePickerScope.description: Pre-arm a browser file-picker dialog so a triggered file chooser is fed a known file path.
NBrowserFilePickerScope.use_when: User clicks a button inside the scope that opens a file-upload dialog. For JS alert/confirm dialogs use NBrowserDialogScope.

NCheckElement.description: Assert a property of a UI element (visible/enabled/text equals/contains) and return a Boolean result.
NCheckElement.use_when: User wants to verify an element's property as a Boolean (e.g. for conditions or assertions). To set a checkbox use NCheck; to test presence only use NCheckState.

NClickTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when a configured UI element is clicked.
NClickTrigger.use_when: User wants an event-driven workflow that reacts to a click on a target UI element. For keyboard-event triggers use NKeyboardTrigger.

NClosePopup.description: Close a transient popup window or notification by clicking its dismiss control.
NClosePopup.use_when: User has a recurring popup blocking automation and wants it auto-dismissed. For ordinary windows use NWindowOperation.

NDragAndDrop.description: Drag a UI element from a source location to a target location with the mouse held down.
NDragAndDrop.use_when: User wants drag-and-drop semantics (reordering, moving items). For ordinary clicks use NClick.

NElementScope.description: Scope that pins a UI element as the parent target for nested activities, avoiding repeated selectors.
NElementScope.use_when: User runs several activities against the same element and wants to share its selector once. For application-level scoping use NApplicationCard.

NExtractFormDataGeneric.description: Extract form-field values from a captured form using AI (wizard-only).
NExtractFormDataGeneric.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to configure NExtractFormDataGeneric through Studio's Form Extractor wizard. To fill rather than read, use NFillForm.

NFillForm.description: Fill multiple form fields in a single call using an AI-powered field mapping.
NFillForm.use_when: User wants to populate several inputs at once given a dictionary of field-name to value. For one field at a time use NTypeInto.

NFindElements.description: Find all UI elements that match a selector and return them as a collection.
NFindElements.use_when: User wants to enumerate all matching elements (e.g. all rows or links). For a single element rely on the implicit find of NClick/NGetText; to iterate use NForEachUiElement.

NForEachUiElement.description: Iterate over each UI element matching a selector and run a body sequence against it.
NForEachUiElement.use_when: User wants to act on every element that matches (each row, each link). For non-UI collections use ForEach; to merely collect them use NFindElements.

NGetBrowserData.description: Read structured data (tables, lists, attributes) from a browser DOM into a typed output.
NGetBrowserData.use_when: User wants to scrape structured data out of a web page in modern UI Automation. For visible plain text use NGetText.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The data is rendered text on a UI element rather than browser storage/cookies/form values.
The data has already been copied to the clipboard rather than living in browser state.

NGetClipboard.description: Read the current contents of the system clipboard into a string variable.
NGetClipboard.use_when: User wants to capture what was just copied. To set the clipboard use NSetClipboard.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The value lives inside a specific UI element rather than on the system clipboard.
You need browser-side data (storage, cookies) instead of generic clipboard contents.

NHighlight.description: Briefly draw a coloured outline around a UI element for debugging or demos.
NHighlight.use_when: User wants to visually highlight an element (debugging, screen recordings). Has no functional effect on the target.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You need to actually trigger hover-state UI (tooltip, dropdown) rather than just outline the element for debugging.
You want a captured image artifact for evidence/logging rather than a live on-screen overlay.

NInjectJsScript.description: Execute a JavaScript snippet in the context of the active browser tab.
NInjectJsScript.use_when: User wants to run custom JS against the page (set values, read DOM, fire events) when a UI activity is insufficient. For ordinary form fills use NTypeInto / NFillForm.

NKeyboardTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when a configured keyboard shortcut is pressed.
NKeyboardTrigger.use_when: User wants an event-driven workflow that reacts to a hotkey. For mouse-click triggers use NClickTrigger.

NNavigateBrowser.description: Navigate the active browser to a URL or perform Back/Forward/Refresh/Home/Close commands.
NNavigateBrowser.use_when: User wants the current browser to change page or perform a navigation command. To open a brand-new browser use NApplicationCardOpen with a URL.

NSAPCallTransaction._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPCallTransaction.description: Invoke an SAP transaction code in the active SAP GUI session.
NSAPCallTransaction.use_when: User wants to jump SAP to a transaction (e.g. /nVA01). Pair with NSAPLogin/NSAPLogon for session setup.

NSAPClickPictureOnScreen._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPClickPictureOnScreen.description: Click a picture/icon element rendered on screen by SAP GUI.
NSAPClickPictureOnScreen.use_when: User has an SAP picture target that NClick cannot resolve. For ordinary controls prefer NClick.

NSAPClickToolbarButton._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPClickToolbarButton.description: Click a button on the SAP GUI toolbar by its accessible identifier.
NSAPClickToolbarButton.use_when: User wants to invoke a toolbar action in SAP GUI by ID rather than by selector. For arbitrary clicks use NClick.

NSAPExpandALVHierarchicalTable._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPExpandALVHierarchicalTable.description: Expand all nodes of an SAP ALV hierarchical table for full-data scraping.
NSAPExpandALVHierarchicalTable.use_when: User wants to read every row of a collapsed SAP ALV hierarchical grid. For tree controls use NSAPExpandALVTree or NSAPExpandTree.

NSAPExpandALVTree._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPExpandALVTree.description: Expand all nodes in an SAP ALV tree control.
NSAPExpandALVTree.use_when: User wants every branch of an SAP ALV tree opened. For non-ALV trees use NSAPExpandTree.

NSAPExpandTree._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPExpandTree.description: Expand every node in an SAP GUI tree control.
NSAPExpandTree.use_when: User wants every branch of a generic SAP tree opened. For ALV trees use NSAPExpandALVTree.

NSAPLogin._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPLogin.description: Log in to an SAP system through the SAP GUI login screen.
NSAPLogin.use_when: User wants automated login against the SAP GUI logon screen. For session selection from SAP Logon Pad use NSAPLogon.

NSAPLogon.description: Open a session from the SAP Logon Pad by its connection name.
NSAPLogon.use_when: User wants to start an SAP session from SAP Logon by connection name. To enter credentials on the SAP login screen itself use NSAPLogin.

NSAPReadStatusbar._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPReadStatusbar.description: Read the SAP GUI status bar message (text and severity) into output variables.
NSAPReadStatusbar.use_when: User needs to capture the status-bar feedback after an SAP transaction (success/warning/error message).

NSAPSelectDatesInCalendar._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPSelectDatesInCalendar.description: Select a date range in the SAP GUI calendar control.
NSAPSelectDatesInCalendar.use_when: User wants to choose dates in an SAP calendar widget. For ordinary dropdowns use NSelectItem.

NSAPSelectMenuItem._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPSelectMenuItem.description: Select a menu entry from the SAP GUI menu bar by its menu path.
NSAPSelectMenuItem.use_when: User wants to invoke a menu-bar action in SAP GUI by path. For toolbar buttons use NSAPClickToolbarButton.

NSAPTableCellScope._sap_note: Must be used inside an NSAPLogon scope. Generator emits a warning if no SAP session context is present.
NSAPTableCellScope.description: Scope that pins an SAP table cell as the target for nested activities.
NSAPTableCellScope.use_when: User wants to perform several actions against the same SAP table cell without repeating the row/column index.

NSetBrowserData.description: Write a structured value (table/list/attribute) back into a browser DOM target.
NSetBrowserData.use_when: User wants to push structured data into the page model. To read use NGetBrowserData.

NSetClipboard.description: Set the system clipboard contents to a given string.
NSetClipboard.use_when: User wants to seed the clipboard before a paste-style action. To read what is on the clipboard use NGetClipboard.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You want the value entered keystroke-by-keystroke into a specific UI field rather than parked on the system clipboard.
You want the value committed directly to a specific element without a paste step.

NSetFocus.description: Move keyboard focus to a UI element without clicking it.
NSetFocus.use_when: User wants the next typed input to land on a specific element without firing a click. For classic activities use SetFocus.

NSetRuntimeBrowser.description: Switch the runtime browser used by subsequent UI Automation activities (Chrome, Edge, Firefox).
NSetRuntimeBrowser.use_when: User wants to override the browser used by the current run (e.g. to force Edge for a specific flow).

NSetText.description: Set the value of a text field directly (paste-style), bypassing per-character typing.
NSetText.use_when: User wants to fill a long string into a field without keystroke simulation (faster, no IME issues). For per-character typing use NTypeInto.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The target requires real keystrokes (e.g. fires onkeypress handlers, has masking, or rejects programmatic value assignment).
You will paste the value yourself and just need to load the clipboard, without writing into a specific element.

NSetValue.description: Update a UI element value using AI-driven element resolution (wizard-only).
NSetValue.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to configure NSetValue through Studio's UpdateUIElementUsingAI wizard. For deterministic typing use NTypeInto.

NTakeScreenshot.description: Capture a screenshot of the target UI element or full screen and store it in a variable.
NTakeScreenshot.use_when: User wants to save a screen capture to disk or memory mid-run. For composite take-and-save flows consider gen_take_screenshot_and_save.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You only need an ephemeral on-screen indicator for debugging, not a saved image.
You need to trigger the application's hover behavior rather than capture the screen.

NUITask.description: Run a high-level UI task described in natural language using AI to choose actions (UITasksWithAI).
NUITask.use_when: User wants AI to plan and execute a multi-step UI flow from a textual instruction. For deterministic single steps use the explicit modern verbs (NClick / NTypeInto).

NUnblockUserInput.description: Re-enable user mouse and keyboard input previously blocked by NBlockUserInput.
NUnblockUserInput.use_when: User wants to release input after an NBlockUserInput. Always run in a Finally to avoid stranding the user.

NWindowOperation.description: Apply a window operation (Maximize, Minimize, Restore, Close, Move, Resize) to a UI element's window.
NWindowOperation.use_when: User wants to manipulate a window's state. For just hiding use HideWindow; for popups use NClosePopup.

Activate.description: Bring an existing UI element's window to the foreground.
Activate.use_when: User wants to focus a window before interacting with it (classic activity). In modern projects prefer NApplicationCardAttach.

CVScope.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

CVScope.description: Computer Vision scope that captures the screen once and serves child Cv* activities from the cached model.
CVScope.use_when: User wants Computer Vision-based UI automation (OCR + CV anchors). All Cv* activities must run inside this scope.

CvExtractDataTableWithDescriptor.description: Extract a tabular region from a Computer Vision capture using a saved descriptor.
CvExtractDataTableWithDescriptor.use_when: User has a CV-anchored table descriptor and wants to scrape its rows. Must be inside CVScope.

CvGetTextWithDescriptor.description: Read text from a Computer Vision target using a saved descriptor.
CvGetTextWithDescriptor.use_when: User wants to capture text from a CV-anchored region. Must be inside CVScope; for selector-based reads use NGetText.

CvHighlightWithDescriptor.description: Briefly highlight a Computer Vision target using a saved descriptor (debug aid).
CvHighlightWithDescriptor.use_when: User wants to visually verify a CV anchor during development. Must be inside CVScope.

CvHoverWithDescriptor.description: Hover over a Computer Vision target using a saved descriptor.
CvHoverWithDescriptor.use_when: User wants hover-only behaviour on a CV-anchored element. Must be inside CVScope.

CvTypeIntoWithDescriptor.description: Type text into a Computer Vision target using a saved descriptor.
CvTypeIntoWithDescriptor.use_when: User wants to type into a CV-anchored field. Must be inside CVScope; for selector-based input use NTypeInto.

FindChildren.description: Return all child UI elements of a parent that match a selector filter.
FindChildren.use_when: User wants to enumerate the children of a known container in classic UIA. In modern UIA prefer NFindElements / NForEachUiElement.

GetActiveWindow.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

GetActiveWindow.description: Return the UI element representing the currently focused foreground window.
GetActiveWindow.use_when: User wants a handle to whatever window the user is currently looking at (classic activity).

GetOCRText.description: Run OCR against a UI element or image region and return the recognised text.
GetOCRText.use_when: User wants OCR text from a region without the modern engine wrappers. For modern OCR pick GoogleCloudOCR / GoogleOCR / MicrosoftAzureComputerVisionOCR.

HideWindow.description: Hide a UI element's window without closing it.
HideWindow.use_when: User wants to push a window off-screen but keep it running. To close use NWindowOperation with Close.

LoadImage.description: Load an image file from disk into a UiPath.Core.Image variable for later CV/OCR use.
LoadImage.use_when: User wants to feed a saved screenshot into Computer Vision or OCR activities. To save instead use SaveImage.

SaveImage.description: Save a UiPath.Core.Image variable to disk as a PNG/JPG file.
SaveImage.use_when: User wants to persist a captured screenshot or CV image to disk. For the inverse use LoadImage; to capture in one step consider gen_take_screenshot_and_save.

SelectMultipleItems.description: Select multiple items from a list-box or multi-select dropdown by visible text.
SelectMultipleItems.use_when: User wants to pick several values from a multi-select control. For single-value picks use NSelectItem.

SetFocus.description: Move keyboard focus to a UI element (classic activity) without clicking.
SetFocus.use_when: User wants to focus an element in classic UIA. In modern projects prefer NSetFocus.

SetWebAttribute.description: Set the value of a named DOM attribute on a web UI element.
SetWebAttribute.use_when: User wants to write directly to a DOM attribute (value, style) bypassing user-style interactions. For ordinary form fills use NTypeInto / NSetText.
